predictions.text: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping will hold an in-person bilateral meeting in China (mainland) that is publicly confirmed by the White House or China’s MFA.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by 2026-05-31, an official White House readout/transcript/photos or an official China MFA/Xinhua report confirms an in-person Trump–Xi meeting occurring on Chinese mainland territory. Virtual meetings do not count.
monitoring_signals: White House schedule releases; NSC/State travel announcements
China MFA press briefings; Xinhua/People’s Daily confirmation
Air Force One tail-number movements; NOTAMs and VIP airport closures in Beijing/Shanghai
G20/APEC/major summit sideline scheduling (if applicable)
predictions.rationale: Public reporting indicates a planned May meeting; execution risk remains from security, scheduling, or escalation in trade/tech disputes.

predictions.text: USPS will implement (effective date reached) a fuel surcharge of at least 8% for at least one domestic package/shipping product, as stated in a USPS public notice or PRC filing.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by 2026-06-30, USPS publishes a notice (or Postal Regulatory Commission docket document) showing a fuel surcharge rate ≥8% that becomes effective for at least one USPS domestic package product (e.g., Ground Advantage, Priority Mail). Announcement without the effective date occurring by the deadline does not count.
monitoring_signals: Postal Regulatory Commission (PRC) dockets for USPS price changes
USPS Industry Alert emails and Postal Bulletin updates
USPS.com price notices and fuel surcharge tables
Major shipper commentary (Pitney Bowes, Stamps.com, Shippo) on USPS surcharge implementation
predictions.rationale: USPS has floated an 8% fuel surcharge amid oil-driven cost pressure; implementation timing is uncertain but plausible within a quarter.

predictions.text: BLS CPI-U headline inflation (seasonally adjusted) will print at or above +0.60% month-over-month in at least one monthly CPI release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any CPI news release published by BLS with reference month April 2026, May 2026, or June 2026 reports CPI-U (all items) SA MoM ≥ +0.60%. Use the first-published value (not revisions).
monitoring_signals: Weekly gasoline prices and crack spreads
Cleveland Fed inflation nowcast; NY Fed CPI core goods/services trackers
Transportation services and energy index components in CPI previews
Dollar strength and import-price prints
predictions.rationale: Energy and shipping cost shocks can push a high headline print, but base effects and shelter disinflation could offset.

predictions.text: ICE Brent front-month futures will settle at or above $115.00 per barrel on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by 2026-05-31, the official ICE settlement price for Brent Crude Futures (front-month at the time) is ≥ $115.00/bbl on any trading day.
monitoring_signals: UKMTO/US Navy incident reports near Hormuz; tanker AIS anomalies
Brent time spreads (backwardation steepening) and implied vol
OPEC+ statements and emergency meetings
SPR policy signals; refinery outage news
predictions.rationale: Hormuz risk can generate sharp upside spikes, but demand weakness or de-escalation could cap prices.

predictions.text: Freddie Mac’s weekly Primary Mortgage Market Survey will report the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage average at or above 7.50% in at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any Freddie Mac PMMS weekly release dated on or before 2026-06-30 reports the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage average ≥ 7.50%.
monitoring_signals: 10-year Treasury yield and MBS OAS/basis moves
Agency MBS supply (Fed QT pace) and bank demand
Mortgage rate lock indices and lender rate sheets
Energy-driven inflation surprises affecting term premium
predictions.rationale: Rates are already elevated and sensitive to inflation/oil shocks, but agency buying and risk-off rallies could prevent a 7.5% print.

predictions.text: Federal Reserve H.4.1 will show the ‘deferred asset’ line item exceeding $250 billion on at least one weekly balance sheet release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any weekly Federal Reserve Statistical Release H.4.1 dated on or before 2026-06-30 reports ‘Deferred asset’ > $250,000,000,000 (strictly greater than $250B).
monitoring_signals: Weekly H.4.1 releases (deferred asset trajectory)
IOER/SOFR levels vs System Open Market Account income
Fed earnings remittance commentary and QT pace changes
Money-market rate persistence and reserve levels
predictions.rationale: With high administered rates, deferred assets can keep rising; crossing $250B depends mainly on rate path and portfolio run-off speed.

predictions.text: USTR will open (announce) a new Section 301 investigation into trade practices affecting U.S. imports, naming China and covering at least one consumer-goods category.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by 2026-08-31, USTR publishes a Federal Register notice or official USTR press release initiating a new Section 301 investigation that explicitly names China and indicates scope including at least one consumer-goods product category. Reviews of existing 301 actions do not count unless explicitly a new investigation initiation.
monitoring_signals: USTR Federal Register postings and press releases
White House trade policy statements post–Supreme Court ruling
Industry petitions (e.g., steel, autos, consumer electronics)
Congressional hearings and letters advocating new trade actions
predictions.rationale: If executive tariff authority is constrained, shifting toward Section 301 is a plausible workaround, but diplomatic talks could delay escalation.

predictions.text: Meta or Alphabet will disclose a litigation settlement accrual/charge of at least $250 million specifically tied to social-media addiction/harm-to-minors lawsuits (not CSAM) in an SEC filing.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a 10-Q, 10-K, or 8-K filed by Meta Platforms, Inc. or Alphabet Inc. on or before 2026-12-31 includes a disclosed litigation accrual/charge ≥ $250,000,000 explicitly tied to social-media addiction, design-related harm, or youth mental-health/addiction claims (excluding CSAM-specific matters).
monitoring_signals: Quarterly filings: legal contingencies and accrual footnotes
Appeal outcomes and post-trial motions in major addiction verdicts
Settlement announcements by state AG coalitions or plaintiffs’ steering committees
Insurance coverage disputes and reserve-building commentary on earnings calls
predictions.rationale: A negligence verdict raises pressure to reserve or settle, but appeals and case fragmentation make timing and accounting recognition uncertain.
